# Python pass parity

`py_pass_parity_test.go` directly consumes `py_pass_golden.json`. The snapshot
is generated by the real optimizer in
`sonolus.py@1040bc0dcc116efdbca05f144edec302e839bcd3`.

`pipeline_fixture.json` is the neutral CFG source consumed by both
`harness_pipeline.py` and the Go parity test. Schema v4 records every checkpoint
as both canonical text and structured CFG, together with the complete
`STANDARD_PASSES` output, ToSSA, first SCCP cleanup, second SCCP, FromSSA, and
Allocate checkpoints.

The common canonical form uses reverse-postorder block IDs, first-definition
local/SSA versions, predecessor-ordered Phi arguments, and source-order
expression arguments. Every checkpoint is compared structurally. Exact
equivalent differences live in `py_pass_allowlist.json`; unknown and unused
entries fail. Final trees are additionally executed over the fixture input
matrix and compared by semantic memory and ordered effects.

Regenerate after deliberately updating the pinned Python baseline:

```powershell
& internal/compiler/testdata/optimize/regenerate.ps1 -PythonCheckout ..\sonolus.py
```

Normal Go tests only consume the checked-in snapshot and do not require Python
or an adjacent checkout.
